mechanism: Landing zone provisioning draws from a 'subscription bank' before creating a new subscription. Subscriptions enter the bank on decommission. New subscriptions created only when the bank is empty.
why: Microsoft Customer Agreement subscription quota is finite. Always creating new subscriptions ties quota to total provisioning count, not active count — decommissioned subscriptions still count against the ceiling.

related.note: Reused subscription must move to the application's invoice section before provisioning completes.
Subscription eligible for the bank only when empty — residual resources violate self-containment.
deleteAll guarantees a decommissioned subscription is empty — makes reuse safe.

violations: New subscription created when bank had an available empty subscription.
Subscription returned to bank with residual resources.
Reused subscription under platform invoice section instead of application's.
